title: Deterministic Tests, Fault Injection, Contract Evidence, and Observability · Automation and Data Client S… · Behavior Case: Fault Injection
summary: A data client needs injected openers, local HTTP fixtures, fake clocks, malformed and partial bodies, contract checks, and privacy-safe telemetry. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http py fault injection increment in a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers fault-injected client verification harness. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inject cancellation, timeout, reset, partial body, malformed fields, bad media type, redirect, and retry exhaustion deterministically. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Verify documented provider and consumer contracts against changed schemas, status codes, and version boundaries. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")

steps.title: Python Predict Fault Injection · Theory Fault Injection
steps.instruction:
theory case — Python protocol evidence model test P-4 injects a delayed header, truncated gzip body, malformed JSON, cross-origin redirect, and cancellation. Assert the outgoing request, cleanup, failure taxonomy, attempt count, low-cardinality telemetry, and no secret leakage. Competency probe: Inject cancellation, timeout, reset, partial body, malformed fields, bad media type, redirect, and retry exhaustion deterministically. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
